items.park_reason: Hand-coded GTE-3x3 matrix transform; target asm annotates every cop2 op 'handwritten instruction'; intricately interleaved (cycle N+1 mtc2 setup happens during cycle N's mvmva, cycle N mfc2 readout happens between cycle N+1 mtc2 setups) with hardcoded swc2 source register \ in the final store. The 2026-05-12 successful match used the gte-3x3.md recipe (register-asm pins t0-t8/v0, single-instruction lui mask materialization in , __asm__ memory barriers, __asm__ nop for load-delay). The 2026-05-31 expanded cheat catalog reclassified all of those as forbidden. Gate routes ASM-PARTIAL (23/67 canonical-asm insns) so neither auto-authorize bucket applies (not a pure GTE leaf wrapper per gte-wrapper-misroute-park.md; not jtbl-infra). Sandbox cannot score (display.c cheat-asm strip truncates pipeline via sibling reorder rule). ESCALATE policy: either (A) authorize as ASM-WHOLE canonical-body asm (hand-coded GTE wrapper - target's 'handwritten instruction' annotation supports this), (B) grandfather the gte-3x3.md recipe cheats as load-bearing for the hand-coded GTE wrapper cluster, or (C) pure-C rework. Cannot mechanically confirm under existing auto-confirm categories.

Canonical jump-table infrastructure, not a register/codegen cheat. The 24 asmfix rules (15 renames of GCC .L labels to external jtbl_800108CC + 8 jlabel conversions + 1 delete_between stripping GCC's emitted table) are structurally required: the switch's jump table jtbl_800108CC sits in the FIRST rodata block (asm/data 800.rodata_pre.o, bb2.ld:25, addr 0x800108CC), while code6cac_b2.o's own .rodata links far later (bb2.ld:50). A GCC in-object jump table can only land in code6cac_b2's late rodata, never at 0x108CC, so referencing the external jtbl symbol + deleting GCC's table is mandatory. Zero-asmfix COMPLETED-C would need relocating the jtbl into code6cac_b2.c AND reordering the whole global rodata layout (project-wide, needs user sign-off). This is the project standard for the 26 jtbl-rename functions whose tables splat carved into asm/data rodata. Separately, sandbox cannot score this file: file-wide cheat-asm stripping shifts maspsx indices and crashes a sibling fn's index-based regfix reorder rule (KeyError in tools/regfix.py).

register-alloc coupling — arg pseudos win allocno tiebreaker for low callee-saves (creation order), forcing rotation. chain-extender already present, volatile-bump regresses (cost > saved). same mechanism as cpu_side_move_dir_4 at larger surface; full pinning in register-alloc-pure-c.md.
